items.text: The PDF changelog export now features a styled layout matching the email digest: a blue header banner, color-coded section headings with emoji icons, and tinted item backgrounds.

Mobile responsive improvements: all data tables now scroll horizontally on narrow screens instead of overflowing, dialogs use tighter margins on small phones so more content is visible, and the Bulk Check-in window resizes to fit the viewport on mobile devices.

Profile Activity — Favorite Nets drill-down: clicking any net name in the Favorite Nets section now expands a session list showing every individual net from that schedule that you have attended, with dates and a direct link to the net report.

Profile Activity — Stat card drill-down: the four activity summary cards (Total Check-ins, Nets Joined, As NCS, Last 30 Days) are now clickable. Clicking a card expands a list of the underlying nets so you can see exactly which sessions make up each number and jump directly to any net report.

Maintenance Banner: Admins can now display a sitewide warning banner from the new Maintenance tab in the Admin panel. Supports a custom message, dismissible or persistent mode, and an optional scheduled start/end window so the banner appears and clears automatically without manual intervention.

Card grids on the Nets dashboard and Schedule pages now scale from 1 column on mobile up to 6 columns on ultrawide monitors, and always fill available space without leaving empty gaps when fewer cards are present.
Tabs on the Admin and Profile pages now shrink and scroll horizontally on narrow viewports, and support swipe-left/right gestures on mobile to switch between tabs.
FAB buttons (Create, Filter, Archive, Merge) are now a uniform size across all pages.

Archived nets now disappear from the Nets list immediately after archiving. Previously, if you navigated back to the Nets list before the 5-second undo window expired, the net would still appear until you refreshed the page.
Profile photos taken on mobile phones now display upright. Previously, portrait or landscape photos were saved sideways because the server ignored the EXIF orientation tag embedded by the camera.
Emoji reaction toolbar no longer shifts chat message text when you hover. The toolbar now overlays the message without causing a layout reflow.

Check-in list now displays in correct chronological order. When a net is created from a template, all staff are pre-assigned NCS roles at the same timestamp — this was causing up to 5 operators to be promoted above stations who checked in earlier. Only operators who were already checked in before the first non-NCS station joined are now treated as active NCS for display ordering.
Net managers and co-managers can now archive, unarchive, and delete nets they manage. Previously only the direct net owner or a site admin could perform these actions.
Sessions are no longer wiped when the backend briefly restarts during a deploy. The app now only logs you out on an explicit authentication failure, not on transient server errors.
Chat photos now show as "[Photo]" in Net Report and PDF exports. Previously, pasted images appeared as a raw internal JSON payload in the on-screen Net Report page and the exported PDF.

The toolbar on the Net View page is now organized into two clear rows: Net Info (search, map, stats, script, announcements, topic history, info URL) and Net Actions (start, edit, roles, check-in, close, import, export, archive, delete). This prevents the toolbar from spilling into a third row on active nets with many features enabled.
Login sessions now last 30 days and automatically renew — weekly net operators will stay signed in without ever needing to re-authenticate.
Emoji reaction controls are now hidden on closed and archived nets. Existing reaction counts remain visible for historical reference but are no longer interactive.
The Activity Log panel now loads collapsed by default, freeing up screen space especially on mobile. It expands with one click and your preference is remembered for the rest of your session.
Net and schedule cards now truncate long descriptions to three lines with a Show more link, keeping the card grid visually uniform.
The Archived Nets list and the Admin users table now paginate at 25 rows per page, with options for 50 or All. Filters, searches, and sort changes automatically reset to page 1.

The nav bar callsign has been replaced with your avatar. Clicking it opens a menu with Profile, Personal Stats, Admin (if applicable), Dark/Light mode toggle, and Logout — consolidating controls that were previously scattered across the top bar.

Profile photo uploads now include an in-browser crop and zoom step. After selecting a photo, a dialog lets you drag to position and scroll or pinch to zoom before uploading. Only the cropped square is sent to the server, so portrait, landscape, and oddly-framed photos always produce a clean avatar.
Admins can now configure session lifetime and rolling renewal in Admin → Security → Session Settings, without editing server config files.

NCS operators viewing a net they haven't checked into now see two check-in options in the prompt: "Check In as NCS" and "Check In as Participant". Both open the standard check-in dialog instead of scrolling to the inline NCS form.

NCS operators can now toggle their active role directly from the toolbar. The crown icon button steps you down to participant (losing NCS badge and management controls) or back up to NCS — without losing your permanent role assignment. The button is only visible to operators who have an NCS role on the current net and have checked in.

NCS and subscriber reminder emails were firing several hours early and repeating every 15 minutes due to a timezone mismatch and deduplication bug. Fixed by using UTC consistently and storing the full scheduled datetime (not just the date) in the reminder log.

Drag-and-drop rotation reordering — The dotted drag handles in the Rotation Order list are now fully interactive. Grab any row and drag it to a new position to reorder the NCS rotation. Works in both the Net Staff dialog and the standalone Rotation modal.
The up/down arrow buttons now reorder rows instantly without clearing or resizing the dialog. Changes are saved to the server in the background, and a red error toast appears if the sync fails.

Fifth-week operator override — Weekly schedules now support an optional fifth-week operator. When a month has a 5th occurrence of the scheduled weekday, that operator is assigned and the normal rotation pauses, then resumes in sequence on the next normal week.
Schedule settings now include a weekly-only Fifth week operator picker, and schedule rows in Net Staff show a small '5th' badge when this override is active.
NCS and subscriber reminder delivery restored — Fixed a reminder-log field mismatch that prevented 24-hour and 1-hour reminder emails from being recorded and reliably deduplicated.

Strafford County weekly net rotation backfill — Added a one-time migration to restore AA1GM to template #8 rotation after an unintended gap; existing operator order is preserved and AA1GM is appended.
Manager auto-inclusion restored for rotation build — 'Build rotation from staff' now always includes the schedule manager if missing, matching documented 2026.05.20 behavior.

Step Away button — Click the pause icon (⏸️) in your toolbar or the Actions column to temporarily step away without checking out. Click again to return. The button appears only when the net is active or in lobby.

Single-frequency nets are now auto-organized — When a net has only one frequency configured, that frequency is automatically selected and assigned to all check-ins. No manual frequency selection needed.
Better avatar colors — Avatar color palette expanded from 12 to 24 colors and improved seeding (now combines callsign + name), significantly reducing color collisions among users.
Gravatar improvements — Gravatar fallback now shows your name initial instead of your callsign, and server-side validation eliminates 404 errors in the browser console.

Admins, schedule managers, and co-managers can now view the subscriber list for a schedule directly in the Net Staff tab, including callsign, name, and email for everyone who clicked the bell or accepted reminder prompts.
The Net Staff dialog (purple people icon) is now tabbed with dedicated views for Net Control Stations, Rotation Order, Schedule, and Subscribers.
Communication actions added to the Net Staff dialog: Email Staff, Email Subscribers, and Email ALL. Access is limited to admins, net managers, and co-managers/co-owners.
Consolidated the separate email buttons in the Net Staff dialog into a single 'Email' button with a 'To' dropdown, supporting delivery to Net Staff, Net Subscribers, or both.
Email communication is now enabled for schedule templates, allowing managers to email their staff and subscribers directly from the schedule views.
The Close/Cancel button in the Net Staff dialog is now green since modifications are saved immediately.

Raise Hand — Participants can now raise their hand during a net to signal they have comments or questions. Click the hand icon in your toolbar (next to check-out) to raise your hand — it lights up in orange so NCS can see it at a glance.
NCS and loggers can raise or lower any participant's hand directly from the action column in the check-in list. Work through the queue by toggling hands one at a time.
Profile photos now appear next to call signs in check-in rows and chat messages. Avatars are pulled automatically from Gravatar based on your account email — no setup required.
Upload a custom profile photo from your Settings page to override Gravatar. Accepts PNG, JPEG, or WebP images up to 2 MB. Remove it anytime to revert to your Gravatar.
The online presence indicator (green dot) now appears as a badge on the avatar instead of a separate dot alongside your call sign.

Import Check-ins from CSV — Net managers can now import check-ins into any open, closed, or archived net from a CSV file — perfect for logging stations that participated outside ECTLogger (paper logs, other software, or phone check-ins). Open the net, click the green import button in the toolbar, download the Import Template to see the format, fill it in, and upload.

Flexible time formats accepted for CSV imports — M/D/YYYY h:mm AM/PM, 24-hour, ISO, and time-only. Choose your timezone or check 'Assume UTC'. Imported check-ins slot in chronologically alongside existing ones.

Toolbar buttons are now organized into two distinct rows: the top row contains net operations (check in/out, start, close, manage roles), and the bottom row contains net functions (export, import, reports, archive, delete). Tip: hover over any icon button to reveal its function.

Co-Manager promotion — Owners and admins can now promote or demote authorized staff as Co-Managers directly from each staff row using a star icon. Co-Managers share owner-level schedule responsibility for owner-gated actions (ownership transfer, linking nets, merge, and delete).
Chat emoji reactions — Chat messages now support hover reactions (👍 🙂 🙁 ❤️ ✅) with live per-message counts synchronized to all connected clients.
Paste images into chat — You can now paste PNG/JPEG/WEBP images directly into chat. The app uploads, generates thumbnails, embeds inline previews, and opens full images in a lightbox.
NCS quick-entry now supports choosing station status at creation time on both desktop and mobile forms.
Check-in Map PDF export from the map window no longer produces a blank white page. The exporter now captures the live map element so Leaflet tiles and overlays render correctly.
What's New / Changelog PDF exports now preserve emoji reactions (for example 👍 🙂 ❤️ ✅) using inline emoji rendering instead of broken glyph fallback.
Changelog PDF text wrapping now handles mixed text-and-emoji lines more reliably, reducing malformed spacing and symbol corruption in exported files.
Map tiles now render with export-safe CORS settings so Net Report PDF map captures no longer come out blank.
A variable mismatch that prevented 1-hour subscriber reminder emails from sending has been fixed, restoring expected reminder delivery.
Early access now correctly bypasses both schedule account-age and net-participation requirements for users explicitly granted that override.
The Net Staff modal now refreshes immediately after manager transfer, so the add-staff dropdown no longer shows stale options from the previous owner.
Profile now surfaces active GPS-derived live location and provides a one-click clear action, fixing cases where stale live location overrode the intended profile grid square during check-ins.
Away check-ins now render with a distinct yellow highlight, and Mobile check-ins are automatically prioritized near the top of the list after NCS rows.
Create Schedule/Edit Schedule screens now use contextual labels for ad-hoc and one-time nets (Create Net/Edit Net), reducing workflow confusion.
The Net Staff action label now reads 'Create schedule' instead of 'Push staff to schedule' for clearer wording.

The Replacement NCS dropdown when swapping a scheduled net now lists all net staff members and the net manager — previously it only showed people already in the rotation, so eligible operators were missing.
Net Report PDFs are now roughly 75% smaller. The export now uses JPEG compression instead of lossless PNG, so a 30 MB report will typically come out under 5 MB with no visible loss of quality.
Table rows are no longer cut in half at a page boundary. The exporter now detects where each row sits on the canvas and moves the page cut to the gap between rows.
Net Report no longer shows '—' for duration on nets where the go-live timer did not fire. Duration is now calculated from the scheduled start time or first check-in as a fallback.
Closing a net now backfills the start time if it was never recorded, so historical reports remain accurate.

Editing a net's topic or other details via the 'Save for this Net' form no longer shifts the scheduled start time by several hours. The time zone was being applied twice during the save — this is now corrected.
Nets created from a monthly template (e.g. '3rd Thursday') now correctly land on the right date — previously the date calculation ignored the day-of-week and week-of-month settings.
The NCS reminder email now includes a direct link to the waiting net, so the Net Control Station can open and start it without navigating the dashboard.
When a 24-hour NCS reminder is sent, the net is automatically created in Scheduled status so it appears in the Nets list ready to go.
NCS reminder emails were silently failing due to an internal API mismatch. The reminder service now correctly computes the schedule and sends the 24-hour and 1-hour duty reminders.
The Net Schedule page and Active Nets page now have extra scroll room at the bottom so FAB buttons no longer obscure the action buttons on the last card.
When editing a schedule's NCS rotation, the 'Add NCS Operator to rotation' dropdown now only lists the net manager and authorized net staff — not every user on the platform.
The net manager is now always included as an option when building or editing a schedule's NCS rotation.
Net Report maps now always display in light mode (OpenStreetMap tiles), ensuring the printed or exported PDF is always legible regardless of your dark-mode setting.
Net Report maps now show a 'Loading map...' spinner overlay while tiles are being fetched, so it's clear the map is loading rather than appearing blank.
Panel header bars (Chat, Activity Log, check-in table) now use the correct background color in both light and dark mode, matching the page header for better visual consistency.

Activity Log panel — The right sidebar on active nets is now split into two stacked panels: Chat (operator-to-operator conversation) and Activity Log (system events like check-ins and status changes). Both panels can be minimized or popped out independently.
The eyeball toggle for hiding system messages from Chat has been removed — system messages now live exclusively in the Activity Log.
Row-per-event re-check model — Each re-check-in now creates a new database row instead of overwriting the original, preserving the full event history for a net.

Prior check-in rows for the same callsign are shown in the table at reduced opacity (40%). A new toggle in the check-in table header lets you hide duplicate rows and see only the most recent entry per callsign.
Net stats now show three separate chips: Stations (unique active callsigns), Rechecks (total re-check events), and Checked Out.
The Net Report and PDF export now label the check-in log with the event count, unique-station count, and re-check count for easy reference.
Saving your profile with an empty callsign field was failing for new accounts. Blank callsign and GMRS callsign fields are now correctly treated as 'not set'.
Check-in system messages now include the frequency and the callsign of the operator who logged the check-in — for example: "W1CRR has checked in from Springfield — 147.345 MHz FM (logged by KC1JMH)".
Users who hold only a GMRS license now display their GMRS callsign everywhere in the app — in the nav bar, check-in lists, activity log messages, PDF exports, and email reminders.
The Create Net wizard now prevents you from advancing past the Name tab without entering a net name, and past the Frequencies tab without selecting at least one frequency.
The Bulk Check-In tool now supports SKYWARN number, weather observation, feedback, and any custom fields defined for the net.
The map in exported Net Report PDFs now always uses light tiles, regardless of whether you're in dark mode.
Check-in rows no longer split across page breaks in exported Net Report PDFs.
Status chip text and borders are now legible on printed/exported PDFs.
The net header section in exported PDFs now uses a full-width stacked layout so long net names and date/time information are never cut off.
Resolved all TypeScript strict-mode errors across the frontend codebase.

Early schedule access — Admins can now grant individual users early access to schedule creation before their account reaches the minimum age requirement. Look for the timer icon in the Actions column of the Users tab — orange means the account is still under the age limit, click it to grant early access. Click the green timer to revoke it.

Saving the Schedule Creation Limits settings (minimum account age, minimum net participations, maximum per day) was failing silently with a 404 error. Setting the minimum account age to 0 to disable it now also works correctly.
Net owners can now delete their own nets in any state — draft, scheduled, active, or closed — without needing an admin. The trash icon shows up on every card you can manage.

The delete confirmation dialog now spells out exactly what will be lost (check-ins, chat, reports) and offers an Archive Instead button when the net is closed. Buttons are color-coded: blue Cancel, yellow Archive, red Delete Permanently.

For active nets, the delete dialog now offers a Close & Archive button — closes the net normally (emailing the full log to the owner) and immediately archives it, so a manager who wants to keep the record of a test or training run can do it in one click.

Net cards on the Active Nets page and schedule cards on the Scheduler now list the Net Manager and the current/next Net Control Station as separate lines, so it's clear when the manager and the operator on the air are different people.

Save edits back to the schedule — The Edit Net page now has two save buttons: 'Save for this Net' (only affects the current net) and 'Save to Schedule' (pushes your edits back to the parent schedule so future nets inherit the new values). A confirmation dialog lists exactly what will be overwritten.

The Net Staff dialog (when opened from a scheduled net) now has a 'Push staff to schedule' button that copies the net's NCS operators into the schedule's authorized staff list. Operators already on the schedule are skipped, so it's safe to repeat.

Schedules now carry stream URL and announcements. Values you set on the schedule propagate to new nets opened from it, and the new 'Save to Schedule' action can promote in-the-moment edits back to the schedule's defaults.
Fixed a long-standing gap where a schedule's info URL, script, stream URL, and announcements were ignored when opening a net from it. Newly opened nets now inherit those values from the schedule.
Schedule managers who aren't global admins can now add staff and build NCS rotations. Previously the operator picker silently returned no results unless you were an admin.

The schedule editor's Net Staff tab is now a clear two-step workflow: first add Authorized Net Staff (operators who can start and run nets), then optionally build an NCS Rotation order. A 'Build rotation from staff' button populates the rotation in one click.
The Net Staff popup mirrors the editor — Manager, Net Staff, and (for schedules) NCS Rotation now appear in a single scrollable Manage view instead of three separate tabs.
Operator pickers now show a 'No other users available' message when empty, so failures stop being silent.
Guest viewers (not logged in) now see only callsign and first name for net managers and NCS — surnames and email addresses are no longer exposed on public net and template listings.

Changelog download buttons — Two new download icons in the What's New dialog: the page icon downloads just the latest version's changelog as a PDF, the open-book icon downloads the entire changelog history.

What's New email digest — A new opt-in on your Profile page sends a single 8 AM digest the morning after each release summarizing all new features and fixes from the prior day. Silent on days with no updates. Off by default.
Subscribe button in the What's New dialog turns the digest on (or off) in one click — no need to dig into Profile settings.
Digests fire at 8 AM in your local timezone (auto-detected from your browser the first time you subscribe), with America/Los_Angeles as a fallback if your timezone isn't set.
Every What's New email includes a one-click unsubscribe link that opts you out of just the digest — your other notification preferences are untouched.

Active staff members and NCS rotation members can now manage a schedule's staff list and rotation, not just the owner. Previously the 'Manage Staff' and 'Manage Rotation' tabs were hidden from anyone who wasn't the owner or an admin.
Fixed permission inconsistency where the NCS rotation/staff endpoints rejected active staff members even though the rest of the template management endpoints accepted them.

The 'Owner / Default NCS' selector moved from the Basic Info tab to the Net Staff tab on the Edit Schedule page, where it is now labeled 'Schedule Manager' — keeping the Manager and the rotation in one place.
The Net Staff modal (opened from a schedule card) now lets the current Manager or an admin transfer ownership inline. Click the pencil next to the Manager name to pick a new operator.
Renamed 'Host' to 'Manager' throughout the schedule and net pages to match standard ham-radio Net Manager terminology.
The Manager (schedule owner) is always implicitly listed as an authorized Net Control Station — they don't need to be added to the staff list to run nets.
Status dropdowns on the check-in list now show a text label next to each emoji icon (e.g., 👂 Listening only, 📢 Announcements) so new NCS users don't have to memorize the icon legend.
Mobile net header is more compact: the duration chip drops the 'Duration:' prefix, the edit-times pencil moved off the header, and toolbar buttons shrink to fit on a single row.
Mobile 'New Check-in' form is now collapsible and starts collapsed — attendees of nets they aren't logging no longer have to scroll past a tall form.

Schedule Statistics overhaul — The schedule statistics page now has time-window filters (30 days, 90 days, 1 year, all-time) and a new Leaderboards tab showing top check-ins, NCS operators, Loggers, and Relay stations — each scoped to the selected time window.
Net History log on schedule stats now shows the NCS callsign(s) for each net instance.
Export to PDF button on schedule statistics for a one-click performance report.

Link Existing Net to Schedule — Schedule owners and admins can now attach an ad-hoc or misfiled net to a schedule from the schedule statistics page so its check-ins count toward the schedule's history.

Schedule merge now correctly preserves all nets and their check-ins under the surviving schedule. Previously, merging schedules could silently detach nets so their stats no longer counted toward the merged schedule.
Replaced the strict 'Regular Operators (50%+)' view with a top-20 check-in leaderboard so long-running nets with operator turnover always show meaningful results.
Schedule cards on the Scheduler page now stretch to equal heights within a row and have a minimum height for a more uniform, professional appearance.
Schedule statistics now defaults to the last 1 year so monthly nets and occasional SKYWARN activations always show meaningful counts. The 30/90 day and all-time filters are still available.
PDF export for schedule statistics now includes all four leaderboards (Check-ins, NCS, Logger, Relay) instead of only the tab that was visible on screen.

Merge Schedules — Combine multiple net schedules into one. All nets, subscribers, staff, and NCS rotation members are moved to the master schedule. Accessible via the merge button on the Scheduler page.
Auto-start nets — Lobby nets now automatically go live at their scheduled start time. NCS can still start early manually.
Edit net times — NCS and admins can now adjust the start and end time of active, closed, or archived nets via an edit icon next to the status chip.
Check-in prompt — Authenticated users viewing an active or lobby net are prompted with a notification asking if they'd like to check in, with a one-click Check In button.
Net titles on the Dashboard are now clickable — click a net name to go directly to the net view.
Check-ins count now shows total participants including checked-out stations, with a separate 'Checked Out' chip. Previously, checked-out stations were excluded from the count.
Guest count now shows actual unauthenticated viewers instead of checked-in stations without online presence. No longer inflated by users who navigated away.
Fixed net close/report email not being sent — a JSON parsing issue with field configuration prevented email delivery for some nets.

Net Report PDF export is now dramatically smaller — text and tables are rendered natively instead of as images. Only maps remain as compressed images. Typical file size reduced from ~24 MB to under 1 MB.
PDF filename now includes the net's start date and time (e.g., 'ARES_Net_Report_2026-03-12_1930.pdf') instead of the export date.

Callsign auto-fill now uses the contacts directory — when NCS enters a callsign, name, location, and SKYWARN number are auto-filled from the user's account or from prior check-in history. Fields remain editable.
Admin Contacts tab — manage station contacts, fix misspelled names, add emails, and send invites to create user accounts.
Check-In Map on the Statistics page — shows a geographic map of approximate regions where check-ins have originated (by grid square or state/province), without revealing individual locations.
Logger role now works correctly — loggers can change check-in statuses and use the check-in entry form. Previously, only NCS and above could perform these actions due to a role-name mismatch.
Check-in location map now plots all stations, including those that checked out during the net — checked-out stations still participated and are shown.
Location parser now recognizes full state names (e.g., 'Skowhegan Maine') in addition to abbreviations, so more check-in locations are mapped correctly.
Browser autocomplete disabled on the Name field in check-in forms — prevents browsers from overwriting the platform's own callsign-based name lookup with saved personal data.
What's New dialog now shows the correct local date for changelog entries regardless of timezone. Same-day releases are merged into a single section.

Dual-map view in PDF Report — when check-ins are geographically clustered with a few outliers at a significant distance, the report now shows two side-by-side maps: a zoomed cluster view and a full overview, so neither is lost.
Check-in location map on the Net Statistics page — fills the empty space next to the status chart with a map showing all check-in locations for that net.
Check-in Pace chart on the Net Statistics page — shows cumulative arrivals over time so you can see at a glance whether stations checked in quickly or trickled in throughout the net.
Check-in now works in LOBBY mode — stations can check in as soon as the NCS opens the lobby before the official start time.
Check-in errors now show as in-app notifications instead of browser alert pop-ups, with the actual error detail from the server.
Map zoom no longer snaps back to show all stations when the check-in list updates. Your zoom level and pan position are now preserved after the initial auto-fit.

Per-user Chat System Messages Toggle — users can now hide or show system (activity) messages from the chat via a toolbar icon (left of the pop-out button). Preference is saved to your profile and persists across sessions.
Announcements / General Traffic — Separate field from net script for listing upcoming events and announcements. Visible to all via megaphone icon. Supports Markdown.
Prior Topics Log — Tracks previously used 'Topic of the Week' prompts to avoid repetition. View via history icon for template-based nets.
Audio Stream URL — Add Shoutcast/Broadcastify stream links to nets for in-browser listening.
Unarchive button added directly to the Archived Nets list.
In-App Changelog floating button (What's New) with unread indicator.

Consistent Action Button Colors — All buttons now use consistent colors throughout the UI (blue for view/search, purple for people/staff, orange for stats, green for exports, teal for ICS-309, red for delete/close) making them easier to find.
Net staff members can now create and start nets (not just rotation members).
WebSocket connections now auto-reconnect if disconnected unexpectedly.
Users can now check out their own check-in (previously only NCS/Logger could).
Net closure now immediately updates all connected clients.
Fixed dead WebSocket connections being kept in memory.
Role assignments (NCS, Logger, Relay) are now logged in chat.
Improved map PDF export reliability.
Email unsubscribe links in all notification emails.
Subscription prompt after check-in when a scheduled net closes.
Admin Users list now shows three-tier online status (green/yellow/red).
PDF exports now force light mode for better printing.
Lobby Mode — NCS can start nets early with countdown to official start.
Unarchive Nets — Restore archived nets to closed status.
Net Report (PDF) — Comprehensive multi-page PDF reports for closed nets.
Inline Check-In Editing — Click any row to edit directly.
Countdown Timer — Shows time until scheduled net start.
